mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, consumer spending growth stagnates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows month-over-month decline, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising interest rates, a sustained increase in the industrial production index signals resilient corporate output that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in the preceding quarter, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or predictive accuracy falls below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 70% of months following CPIAUCSL MoM spikes above 0.4%, or inflation fails to predict return direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of expanding real GDP and declining unemployment confirms a broad-based economic recovery, boosting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth is driven solely by inventory accumulation, or the joint signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals an imminent supply-side bottleneck or demand collapse, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive equity returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.
